item.title: 特朗普希望中国和其他国家向霍尔木兹海峡派遣舰船
item.description: “希望中国、法国、日本、韩国、英国以及其他受此人为限制影响的国家能派出军舰，”唐纳德·特朗普总统在Truth Social上发文称。【原文：“Hopefully China, France, Japan, South Korea, the UK, and others, that are affected by this artificial constraint, will send Ships,” President Donald Trump says in a Truth Social post.】

item.title: 马来西亚反贪污委员会主席被指持有数百万股股票，国会议员称
item.description: 一名马来西亚议员表示，对该国反贪污委员会主席阿扎姆·巴基持股情况的调查据称发现，他在九家公司中持有价值1400万林吉特（360万美元）的股份。【原文：A Malaysian lawmaker said a probe into the shareholdings of the country’s anti-graft chief Azam Baki allegedly uncovered that he owned shares worth 14 million ringgit ($3.6 million) in nine companies.】

item.title: 泰晤士水务公司的债权人向英国水务监管局（Ofwat）提交了改进后的救援方案
item.description: 据一位知情人士透露，泰晤士水务公司的债权人已为这家陷入困境的公用事业公司提出了一份改进后的救援方案，其中包括增加股权注资和提高承诺债务额度。【原文：Thames Water’s creditors have pitched an improved rescue offer for the troubled utility that includes a bigger equity injection and an increase in committed debt, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.】

item.title: 霍尔木兹海峡追踪：沙特苏伊士型油轮在孟买近海重新出现；液化石油气运往印度
item.description: 在地区局势紧张加剧的背景下，霍尔木兹海峡的商船交通量仍维持在极低水平，过去24小时内仅有寥寥数艘原油和液化石油气油轮通过该海峡。【原文：Commercial vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz continues at very low levels, with just a handful of oil and liquefied petroleum gas tankers transiting in the last 24 hours amid heightened regional tensions.】

item.title: 第二艘Dynacom油轮通过霍尔木兹海峡，其他船东则暂缓行动
item.description: 一名希腊船东派遣第二艘油轮穿越霍尔木兹海峡，此举与航运业的谨慎态度背道而驰，此前伊朗为报复以色列和美国的袭击，已在该地区发动猛烈反击。【原文：A Greek shipowner sent a second oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz, bucking caution among the shipping industry as Iran lashes out across the region in response to attacks by Israel and the US.】

item.title: 伊朗战争助推神秘韩国大亨押注大型油轮业务
item.description: 随着伊朗战争在全球能源市场引发混乱，一位神秘的韩国大亨的收入却大幅飙升。【原文：As the war in Iran wreaks chaos across global energy markets, one enigmatic Korean tycoon has seen his earnings soar.】

item.title: 私营信贷风险与战争阴云笼罩美国金融股前景
item.description: 金融股迎来了自新冠疫情以来最糟糕的开年表现，投资者预计未来还将面临更多挑战，因为从私营信贷到伊朗战争等各方面引发的担忧正令这个本已举步维艰的行业动荡不安。【原文：Financial stocks are off to their worst start to a year since the Covid pandemic, with investors expecting more pain ahead as worries over everything from private credit to the Iran war roil the troubled sector.】

item.title: 卫星公司称，在空袭后，有两艘油轮被发现在哈尔格岛附近
item.description: 据专门通过分析卫星图像追踪船舶动向的公司Tankertrackers.com称，在美国袭击伊朗关键石油出口设施哈格岛的军事设施数小时后，两艘油轮已停靠在该岛。【原文：Two oil tankers were berthed at key Iranian oil-export facility Kharg Island hours after the US attacked military installations there, according to Tankertrackers.com, a firm that specializes in studying satellite imagery to follow ship movements.】

item.title: 苹果的iPhone 17e是价格更高的iPhone 17的绝佳替代品
item.description: 苹果公司最近对常规版iPhone进行了多项重大升级，为其入门级“e”系列设定了很高的标准。【原文：Having recently updated the regular iPhone with a raft of meaningful upgrades, Apple Inc. set a high bar for its entry-level “e” series.】

item.title: 私募信贷获“黄色预警”，瑞典国防则获“绿灯”
item.description: 私募信贷基金已将目光转向散户投资者，而这些投资者并不习惯在出现问题时资金被冻结。【原文：Private credit funds have turned to retail investors, who are not used to having their money tied up when things go wrong.】

item.title: Mota-Engil称其首席执行官面临Muddy Waters提起的诽谤诉讼
item.description: Mota‑Engil SGPS SA 周六表示，做空机构浑水研究（Muddy Waters）已在美国对该公司及其首席执行官提起民事诽谤诉讼。【原文：Mota‑Engil SGPS SA said on Saturday that short‑seller Muddy Waters has filed a civil defamation lawsuit in the US against the company and its chief executive officer.】

item.title: 受钻石行业困境影响，标准普尔下调博茨瓦纳信用评级
item.description: 标普全球评级于上周五晚间下调了博茨瓦纳的信用评级，虽维持其投资级评级，但指出受钻石行业困境影响，财政稳定性面临风险。该评级机构维持负面展望。【原文：S&P Global Ratings cut Botswana’s credit score late Friday, maintaining its investment grade but citing risks to fiscal stability amid diamond industry woes. The ratings company maintained a negative outlook.】

item.title: 斯科蒂·皮蓬助力华尔街接受预测市场
item.description: 杰夫·斯普雷彻掌管着一家市值900亿美元的公司，该公司拥有纽约证券交易所，制定全球原油和利率基准，且去年交易量创下历史新高。【原文：Jeff Sprecher runs a $90 billion company that owns the New York Stock Exchange, sets global benchmarks for oil and interest rates, and traded record volumes last year.】

item.title: 一艘驶往俄罗斯的希腊油轮在黑海遭到袭击
item.description: 希腊海运部表示，一艘希腊油轮在驶往俄罗斯途中，于黑海遭遇弹药或无人机袭击而受损。【原文：A Greek oil tanker was damaged in a projectile or drone attack in the Black Sea as it sailed toward Russia, Greece’s shipping ministry said.】

item.title: 德国部长呼吁大力发展可再生能源以保障能源安全
item.description: 德国环境部长呼吁该国加快采用可再生能源，以此增强能源安全并减少对化石燃料的依赖。【原文：Germany’s environment minister urged the country to accelerate adoption of renewable power sources as a way to boost its energy security and reduce its reliance on fossil fuel.】

item.title: 伊朗战争使海湾地区的雄心壮志及特朗普的“交易”前景蒙上阴影
item.description: 一位经济学家指出，向美国承诺大量投资背后的隐含条件是“不要让中东局势失控”。【原文：The implicit quid-pro-quo for showering the US with investment pledges, an economist says, was “do not set the Middle East on fire.”】

item.title: 中国人工智能初创公司Moonshot以180亿美元估值成功融资
item.description: Moonshot AI正计划在一轮扩充融资中筹集高达10亿美元的资金，此轮融资将使这家初创企业的估值达到约180亿美元。这意味着其估值在短短三个月内增长了四倍有余，也凸显了业界对中国人工智能开发者日益增长的关注——这些开发者正争相与硅谷领军企业一较高下。【原文：Moonshot AI is seeking to raise as much as $1 billion in an expanded funding round that would value the startup at about $18 billion, more than quadrupling its valuation in just three months and underscoring growing interest in Chinese AI developers racing to rival Silicon Valley leaders.】

item.title: 全球经济图解：伊朗战争爆发第二周，油价突破100美元
item.description: 美国表示，随着席卷中东的战争进入第两周，并继续扰乱能源供应和全球市场，美方已将对伊朗的空袭升级至前所未有的程度。【原文：The US said it had stepped up strikes on Iran to unprecedented levels as the war that’s engulfed the Middle East hit the two-week mark and continued to upend energy flows and global markets.】

item.title: 2026年前两个月，中国国家电网投资大幅增长
item.description: 中国国家电网公司表示，随着北京方面着力稳定经济增长并扩大基础设施支出，该公司正加快对电网的投资。【原文：State Grid Corp. of China said it’s accelerating investment in power grids as Beijing pushes to stabilize growth and expand infrastructure spending.】

item.title: 富查伊拉部分石油作业因无人机袭击及火灾暂停
item.description: 据知情人士透露，位于霍尔木兹海峡入口处的富查伊拉港，部分装油作业因周六上午发生的无人机袭击及火灾而暂停。【原文：Some oil loading operations in the port of Fujairah, just outside the Strait of Hormuz, have been suspended after a drone attack and fire on Saturday morning, people familiar with the matter said.】

item.title: 中国游客在春假期间出游人数创下新高
item.description: 新华社周六报道称，春节期间，中国游客的国内出游人数创下历史新高。【原文：Chinese travelers took a record number of domestic trips during the Spring Festival Travel season, Xinhua News Agency reported Saturday.】

item.title: 两艘液化石油气船驶过霍尔木兹海峡，前往面临供应短缺的印度
item.description: 两艘载有液化石油气的油轮在穿越霍尔木兹海峡后正驶往印度，这在一定程度上缓解了因波斯湾战争导致烹饪燃料供应中断而造成的严重短缺。【原文：Two tankers carrying liquefied petroleum gas are heading to India after crossing the Strait of Hormuz, providing some relief to acute shortages as the war in the Persian Gulf disrupts supply of the cooking fuel.】

item.title: JSW钢铁公司获得莫桑比克一座炼焦煤矿的开发权
item.description: 印度产能最大的钢铁制造商JSW钢铁有限公司赢得了在莫桑比克特特省开发一座煤矿的权利，从而确保了钢铁生产所需的关键原材料的供应。【原文：JSW Steel Ltd., India’s biggest steelmaker by capacity, won rights to develop a coal mine in Mozambique’s Tete province, securing access to a key raw material used in steel manufacturing.】

item.title: 瑞士法郎势不可挡，给出口商带来新的痛苦
item.description: 瑞士企业家克里斯蒂安·坦勒很早就意识到，他必须让自己的企业做好应对瑞郎升值的准备。【原文：Swiss entrepreneur Christian Taennler knew early on that he had to get his business ready for a stronger franc.】

item.title: 俄罗斯称大型炼油厂和重要港口遭乌克兰无人机袭击
item.description: 俄罗斯当局表示，乌克兰无人机袭击引发了一家大型炼油厂起火，并袭击了俄罗斯克拉斯诺达尔边疆区的一个重要港口。【原文：Ukrainian drone attacks sparked a fire at a major oil refinery and struck a key port in Russia’s Krasnodar region, Russian authorities said.】

item.title: 美国袭击哈尔格岛军事设施，伊朗再次发出报复威胁
item.description: 伊朗警告称，如果其基础设施遭到袭击，将对中东地区与美国有关联的石油和能源设施发动袭击。此前，美国轰炸了哈格岛这一关键前哨基地上的军事目标，伊朗再次重申了这一威胁。【原文：Iran warned it will target American-linked oil and energy facilities in the Middle East if its own infrastructure is attacked, reiterating its threat after the US bombed military targets on the critical outpost of Kharg Island.】

item.title: 浙江与吉利深化合作，共建世界级汽车产业集群
item.description: 中国浙江省政府与吉利汽车集团签署了一项新的战略合作协议，双方由此加深了合作关系，该地区正致力于巩固其在全球电动汽车供应链中的地位。【原文：China’s Zhejiang provincial government signed a new strategic cooperation agreement with automobile group Geely, deepening ties as the region seeks to strengthen its position in the global electric‑vehicle supply chain.】

item.title: 澳大利亚农村地区面临“无法接受”的燃油短缺
item.description: 澳大利亚能源部长克里斯·鲍文表示，农村和地区正面临“真实且无法接受的”燃料短缺，这是美以与伊朗冲突引发的连锁反应，该冲突导致全球油价飙升。【原文：Australia’s Energy Minister Chris Bowen says rural and regional areas are facing “real and unacceptable shortages” of fuel, a ripple effect of the US-Israel war with Iran that has caused global oil prices to surge.】

item.title: 伊朗石油枢纽遭袭引发对供应进一步动荡的担忧
item.description: 美国对该岛发动的大规模袭击——该岛出口了伊朗大部分原油——引发了人们对该地区供应中断范围可能进一步扩大的担忧，这给中东地区经历两周战乱后本已动荡不安的石油和天然气市场带来了更大压力。【原文：A major US attack on the island that exports the bulk of Iran’s crude is raising fears of more widespread supply disruptions in the region, further straining oil and gas markets already in upheaval after two weeks of war in the Middle East.】

item.title: GE Vernova与日立将共同在东南亚探索小型反应堆项目
item.description: GE Vernova公司与日立公司达成协议，将共同探讨在东南亚部署小型模块化核反应堆的机遇。【原文：GE Vernova Inc. and Hitachi Ltd. agreed to explore opportunities to deploy small modular nuclear reactors in Southeast Asia.】
